summary: Beautiful soundboard capture of a mid‑80s powerhouse set, highlighted by a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s and thunderous Morning Dew.
review: The recording is a clean soundboard source with crisp mix and strong B3 presence, though a few listeners note occasional feedback and that another board version sounds slightly better. Musically the show shines in the second set – Help/Slip/Franklin’s, Why Don’t We Do It In The Road, Morning Dew and Not Fade Away are repeatedly praised, while the first set is described as uneven.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (8 of 10) give 4‑5 stars and rave about both performance and sound quality; only a few minor complaints about feedback or alternate boards temper the score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp mix with strong B3 roar and overall beautiful sound; occasional loud drum/feedback noted by one reviewer and another mentions a different board sounds better.

standout_songs: Why Don't We Do It In The Road
Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as uneven or lackluster, but the second set builds momentum and delivers several high‑energy peaks, making it the highlight of the night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for incendiary solos, soaring high notes and arguably his best playing despite vocal rasp; steals the show on many jams.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as booming and solid throughout the year.
band_member_comments.Bob: Lauded as a front‑man in peak form, delivering laid‑back yet powerful vocals.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (B3) noted for roaring tones and new sounds that drive the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: One reviewer mentions extreme feedback on drums; Mickey & Bill overall called tight.
